labels.name: area:core
labels.description: Core scanner, inventory, policy engine, and shared types.

labels.name: area:parser
labels.description: Config, manifest, lockfile, or workflow parser.

labels.name: area:reporting
labels.description: Human, JSON, SARIF, Markdown, and annotation output.

labels.name: area:node
labels.description: npm, pnpm, Yarn, Bun, and Node.js dependency controls.

labels.name: area:python
labels.description: pip, uv, Poetry, and Python dependency controls.

labels.name: area:registry
labels.description: Registry, package source, mirror, proxy, and source-mapping controls.

labels.name: area:cooldown
labels.description: Minimum release age, cooldown, and exclude-newer controls.

labels.name: area:lockfiles
labels.description: Lockfile, frozen install, checksum, and integrity controls.

labels.name: area:secrets
labels.description: Token and credential exposure in package-manager config.

labels.name: area:policy
labels.description: PkgWarden policy files, profiles, and org configuration.

labels.name: area:integrations
labels.description: External service integrations and API adapters.

labels.name: profile:private-registry
labels.description: Private registry, artifact proxy, and dependency-confusion hardening profile.

labels.name: size:XL
labels.description: Very large task or epic-like issue.

labels.name: status:blocked
labels.description: Blocked by another issue or external decision.

labels.name: good-first-issue
labels.description: Good first contribution for new maintainers.

milestones.title: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
milestones.description: Create the PkgWarden repo, CLI entrypoint, scanner data model, parser abstraction, fixture harness, and initial reports. This milestone must land before ecosystem-specific rules scale up.

milestones.title: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
milestones.description: Implement npm, pnpm, Yarn, and Bun discovery, parsers, and hardening rules for cooldowns, registries, lockfiles, install scripts, and exotic dependency specs.

milestones.title: M2: Python package-manager hardening MVP
milestones.description: Implement pip, uv, and Poetry discovery, parsers, and hardening rules for package indexes, cooldown/exclude-newer controls, hashes, pinning, lockfiles, and firewall posture.

milestones.title: M3: Dependency bots, CI install validation, and firewall posture
milestones.description: Analyze Dependabot, Renovate, GitHub Actions install commands, and vendor-neutral package firewall/proxy posture. Align bot cooldowns with package-manager hardening.

milestones.title: M4: Reporting, GitHub Action, and releaseable v0.1
milestones.description: Ship production-friendly JSON, Markdown, SARIF, GitHub Action, annotations, documentation, and release workflow for v0.1.

milestones.title: M5: Safe autofix and policy profiles v0.2
milestones.description: Add conservative patch generation, profile-driven enforcement, policy schema validation, and safe autofix for low-risk package-manager and bot settings.

milestones.title: M6: Additional ecosystems v0.3
milestones.description: Expand beyond Node/Python to JVM, .NET, Ruby, Rust, and Go package-manager hardening controls.

milestones.title: M7: Organization scale and plugin system v1.0
milestones.description: Add org-level policy bundles, optional vendor API adapters, plugin interfaces, and large-scale rollout features.

issues.title: PW-001: Create repository scaffold, CLI entrypoint, and project conventions
issues.milestone: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
issues.body:
## Summary
Create the initial PkgWarden repository structure and a working CLI skeleton that can run `pkgwarden scan <path>` without implementing full rule logic yet.

## Scope
- Establish source layout, package/module naming, dependency management, linting, tests, and local development commands.
- Add CLI commands for `scan`, `version`, and `help`.
- Include project metadata, license placeholder, README, and contribution stub.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `pkgwarden scan .` runs and exits successfully against an empty fixture repo.
- [ ] CLI supports `--format human|json` even if output is initially minimal.
- [ ] Unit test and lint commands are documented and pass in CI.
- [ ] README clearly states that PkgWarden is not an SCA/vulnerability scanner.

## Implementation notes
Recommended default stack: Go CLI with a statically compiled binary and a GitHub Action wrapper. If Ozark chooses another stack, preserve the same CLI contract and data model.

## Non-goals
- Do not implement vulnerability database lookup.
- Do not call external vendor APIs in the foundation milestone.

issues.title: PW-002: Define scanner data model for inventory, evidence, findings, rules, and profiles
issues.milestone: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
issues.body:
## Summary
Define the core domain objects that every parser, rule, and reporter will use.

## Scope
- Inventory model for ecosystems, package managers, manifests, lockfiles, CI workflows, dependency bots, and package-manager config files.
- Finding model with id, title, severity, category, confidence, file path, line range, evidence, recommendation, references, and autofix metadata.
- Rule model with id, ecosystem, category, severity, profile applicability, and remediation fields.
- Profile model for baseline, strict, Socket Firewall, Veracode Package Firewall, and private-registry modes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] JSON schema for scan output is committed.
- [ ] Every finding can be traced to one or more evidence locations when possible.
- [ ] Severity and confidence enums are documented.
- [ ] Rules can be enabled/disabled by profile and by explicit policy configuration.

## Implementation notes
Keep the model vendor-neutral. Socket and Veracode should be profiles/integrations, not hard-coded assumptions.

issues.title: PW-003: Implement repository inventory walker
issues.milestone: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement a safe filesystem inventory engine that discovers relevant package-manager, dependency-bot, and CI files without scanning unnecessary directories.

## Scope
- Walk a target repository and identify manifests, lockfiles, package-manager config files, CI workflows, and bot configs.
- Respect ignore directories such as `.git`, `node_modules`, virtualenvs, build outputs, caches, and vendored folders unless explicitly requested.
- Support monorepos with multiple package roots.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Inventory includes file paths, ecosystem guesses, and package-manager guesses.
- [ ] Scanner handles nested workspaces and multiple package managers in one repo.
- [ ] Ignored directories are covered by tests.
- [ ] A missing or unreadable file produces a clear warning rather than a crash.

## Dependencies
- PW-002

issues.title: PW-004: Create parser abstraction and line-aware parse utilities
issues.milestone: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
issues.body:
## Summary
Create reusable parser interfaces for YAML, JSON, TOML, INI-style files, XML, shell-like package configs, and line-oriented requirements files.

## Scope
- Expose parsed values plus source file and line metadata.
- Handle comments and duplicate keys where package-manager semantics require it.
- Return structured parse errors as scanner warnings.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Parsers can return evidence locations for at least JSON, YAML, TOML, INI, XML, and line-oriented text.
- [ ] Parser tests cover malformed input and duplicate/overridden keys.
- [ ] Rules can query config values without knowing the raw file format.

## Dependencies
- PW-002

issues.title: PW-005: Implement rule engine and baseline profile execution
issues.milestone: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement the engine that executes enabled rules against repository inventory and emits normalized findings.

## Scope
- Rule registration and execution order.
- Profile selection via `--profile baseline|strict|socket-firewall|veracode-package-firewall|private-registry`.
- Rule suppression via policy file and inline config where safe.
- Deduplication of repeated findings.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A sample no-op rule and sample failing rule execute through the engine.
- [ ] Profile selection changes enabled rules.
- [ ] Output includes rule id, category, severity, and recommendation.
- [ ] Suppressed findings are tracked separately from active findings.

## Dependencies
- PW-002
- PW-003
- PW-004

issues.title: PW-006: Add fixture repository test harness
issues.milestone: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
issues.body:
## Summary
Create a fixture-based test harness so rules can be tested against realistic repository layouts.

## Scope
- Fixture repos for empty, single-package, monorepo, malformed config, and mixed ecosystem cases.
- Golden JSON output tests for scanner results.
- Utilities for adding small synthetic package-manager examples.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] At least five fixture repos are committed.
- [ ] Golden output tests run in CI.
- [ ] Test harness can run a subset of rules and profiles.
- [ ] Contributors can add a fixture by following documented steps.

## Dependencies
- PW-001
- PW-005

issues.title: PW-007: Implement human and JSON report output
issues.milestone: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement initial scan reporting in human-readable terminal output and machine-readable JSON.

## Scope
- Human summary grouped by severity, ecosystem, and category.
- Full JSON output matching the committed schema.
- Exit-code behavior controlled by severity threshold.
- Redaction of secrets in evidence fields.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `--format human` and `--format json` produce useful output.
- [ ] `--fail-on high` fails when a high-severity finding exists.
- [ ] Output never prints raw package-manager tokens.
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover report formatting.

## Dependencies
- PW-002
- PW-005

issues.title: PW-008: Add `.pkgwarden.yml` policy file support
issues.milestone: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
issues.body:
## Summary
Allow repositories to configure PkgWarden policy, approved registries, selected profiles, rule overrides, and suppressions.

## Scope
- Load `.pkgwarden.yml` from repo root by default.
- Support approved registry endpoints, package firewall endpoints, default cooldown days, rule severity overrides, and explicit rule suppressions.
- Validate policy schema and emit actionable errors.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Example policy file is included.
- [ ] Unknown keys produce warnings or schema errors depending on strictness setting.
- [ ] Policy can enable a profile and override one rule severity.
- [ ] Suppression requires a reason string.

## Dependencies
- PW-002
- PW-005

issues.title: PW-009: Add initial documentation skeleton and non-goals page
issues.milestone: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
issues.body:
## Summary
Create docs that explain what PkgWarden does, what it intentionally does not do, and how users should interpret findings.

## Scope
- README overview.
- Non-goals page.
- MVP rule categories.
- Basic installation and scan examples.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Docs clearly state PkgWarden is a package-manager hardening advisor, not SCA.
- [ ] Docs include a minimal example finding.
- [ ] Docs explain profiles and severity at a high level.

## Dependencies
- PW-001

issues.title: PW-010: Add secret-redaction utility for package-manager evidence
issues.milestone: M0: Project foundation and scanner core
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement shared redaction helpers so package-manager tokens and credentials are not exposed in scan output.

## Scope
- Recognize common token patterns in `.npmrc`, `.pypirc`, `pip.conf`, `poetry.toml`, `nuget.config`, `.yarnrc.yml`, and URLs.
- Redact evidence while preserving enough context to explain the finding.
- Add tests for edge cases.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Raw auth tokens are not emitted in human, JSON, or future SARIF output.
- [ ] Redaction utility is used by all report formats.
- [ ] Tests cover URL credentials, bearer-like values, npm auth tokens, and environment variable placeholders.

## Dependencies
- PW-007

issues.title: PW-011: Detect Node.js package managers, manifests, lockfiles, and workspaces
issues.milestone: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement Node ecosystem inventory for npm, pnpm, Yarn, and Bun.

## Scope
- Detect `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `yarn.lock`, `.yarnrc.yml`, `bun.lock`, `bun.lockb`, and `bunfig.toml`.
- Detect package manager from `packageManager` field, lockfiles, workspace files, and config files.
- Handle monorepos and mixed package managers.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Inventory identifies npm, pnpm, Yarn, and Bun correctly in fixture repos.
- [ ] Workspace roots and package roots are represented separately.
- [ ] Conflicting package-manager signals produce a warning finding.

## Dependencies
- PW-003
- PW-004

issues.title: PW-012: Parse `.npmrc` and npm-related package config
issues.milestone: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Parse npm configuration from repo-level `.npmrc` and relevant package metadata.

## Scope
- Parse keys including `registry`, scoped registries, `min-release-age`, `before`, `ignore-scripts`, `audit`, `package-lock`, and auth-related keys.
- Preserve line numbers and redacted evidence.
- Detect scoped registry mappings and public registry fallback.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Parser handles comments, duplicate keys, scoped keys, and environment-variable values.
- [ ] Rules can query effective config values and evidence locations.
- [ ] Tests cover npm registry, cooldown, and auth-token patterns.

## Dependencies
- PW-004
- PW-010
- PW-011

issues.title: PW-013: Implement npm cooldown and lockfile hardening rules
issues.milestone: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Add npm rules for release-age cooldown and reproducible install posture.

## Scope
- Rule `npm.cooldown.min_release_age`: require or recommend `min-release-age` based on selected profile.
- Rule `npm.cooldown.before_conflict`: warn when `before` and `min-release-age` create ambiguous or stale behavior.
- Rule `npm.lockfile.required`: require `package-lock.json` or `npm-shrinkwrap.json` for npm projects.
- Rule `npm.config.package_lock_not_disabled`: flag `package-lock=false`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Rules emit findings with exact file/line evidence where available.
- [ ] Baseline recommends 7-day cooldown by default; strict can require configurable higher value.
- [ ] Rules are covered by positive and negative fixtures.
- [ ] Recommendations include safe snippets and explain possible install impact.

## Dependencies
- PW-012

issues.title: PW-014: Parse `pnpm-workspace.yaml` and legacy pnpm config locations
issues.milestone: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Parse pnpm project configuration with awareness that modern pnpm project settings belong in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, while older repos may still use `.npmrc`.

## Scope
- Parse `minimumReleaseAge`, `minimumReleaseAgeStrict`, `minimumReleaseAgeExclude`, `strictDepBuilds`, `allowBuilds`, `ignoredBuiltDependencies`, `dangerouslyAllowAllBuilds`, `blockExoticSubdeps`, and `verifyDepsBeforeRun`.
- Detect values in `.npmrc` that should be migrated or treated as legacy.
- Preserve line metadata.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Parser handles boolean, string, number, and list settings.
- [ ] Legacy `.npmrc` values are captured and can produce migration recommendations.
- [ ] Fixtures cover pnpm workspace and non-workspace repos.

## Dependencies
- PW-004
- PW-011

issues.title: PW-015: Implement pnpm cooldown, build-script, exotic dependency, and verification rules
issues.milestone: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Add pnpm rules for minimum release age, dependency script execution, exotic dependency blocking, and dependency verification before run.

## Scope
- Rule `pnpm.cooldown.minimum_release_age`.
- Rule `pnpm.scripts.strict_dep_builds`.
- Rule `pnpm.scripts.no_dangerously_allow_all_builds`.
- Rule `pnpm.scripts.allow_builds_reviewed`.
- Rule `pnpm.exotic.block_exotic_subdeps`.
- Rule `pnpm.verify_deps_before_run`.
- Rule `pnpm.lockfile.frozen_install_ci` placeholder for CI integration.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Rules distinguish missing, weak, and explicitly unsafe settings.
- [ ] Findings explain when a setting may break installs and require review.
- [ ] Positive and negative fixtures cover each rule.
- [ ] Recommendations use `pnpm-workspace.yaml` as the preferred project config location.

## Dependencies
- PW-014

issues.title: PW-016: Parse `.yarnrc.yml` and Yarn project metadata
issues.milestone: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Parse Yarn configuration relevant to dependency hardening.

## Scope
- Parse `npmRegistryServer`, `npmScopes`, `npmMinimalAgeGate`, `npmPreapprovedPackages`, `enableHardenedMode`, `enableImmutableInstalls`, `enableNetwork`, `enableScripts`, `checksumBehavior`, and unsafe HTTP allowlists.
- Detect Yarn Berry vs legacy Yarn where possible.
- Preserve line metadata.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Parser handles nested `npmScopes` registry configs.
- [ ] Parser supports values needed for cooldown, registry, script, and checksum rules.
- [ ] Malformed YAML produces scanner warning, not crash.

## Dependencies
- PW-004
- PW-011

issues.title: PW-017: Implement Yarn cooldown, hardened mode, immutable install, script, and checksum rules
issues.milestone: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Add Yarn rules that check minimum package age, immutable installs, hardened mode, third-party script execution posture, and checksum behavior.

## Scope
- Rule `yarn.cooldown.npm_minimal_age_gate`.
- Rule `yarn.hardened_mode.pr_context`.
- Rule `yarn.install.immutable_ci` placeholder for CI validation.
- Rule `yarn.scripts.enable_scripts`.
- Rule `yarn.checksum.behavior`.
- Rule `yarn.registry.scoped_mappings`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Rules identify missing or unsafe Yarn settings.
- [ ] Findings distinguish local developer recommendations from CI-specific requirements.
- [ ] Fixtures include Yarn Berry and legacy Yarn examples.
- [ ] Recommendations include `.yarnrc.yml` snippets.

## Dependencies
- PW-016

issues.title: PW-018: Parse Bun config and implement Bun hardening rules
issues.milestone: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Support Bun package-manager hardening checks.

## Scope
- Parse `bunfig.toml` install settings such as registry, minimum release age, frozen lockfile, and trusted dependencies.
- Detect `bun.lock` or `bun.lockb`.
- Implement rules for cooldown, trusted dependency scripts, and lockfile/frozen install posture.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Bun projects are discovered and reported.
- [ ] Rules emit findings for missing minimum release age and broad trusted dependency allowances.
- [ ] Fixtures cover safe and unsafe Bun configs.

## Dependencies
- PW-011
- PW-004

issues.title: PW-019: Detect exotic and high-risk dependency specifiers in Node manifests
issues.milestone: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Flag dependency declarations that bypass normal registry controls, such as git URLs, HTTP tarballs, local file paths, and unpinned aliases.

## Scope
- Inspect dependencies, devDependencies, optionalDependencies, peerDependencies, overrides/resolutions where applicable.
- Classify git, GitHub shorthand, HTTP(S) tarball, file/link/workspace, npm alias, and unbounded version ranges.
- Allow policy-based exceptions.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Findings include package name, specifier type, manifest path, and line if available.
- [ ] Workspace references are not treated as unsafe by default.
- [ ] Policy can allow specific packages or specifier classes with a reason.

## Dependencies
- PW-011
- PW-005
- PW-008

issues.title: PW-020: Implement Node registry, firewall, and private-registry posture checks
issues.milestone: M1: Node.js package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Validate whether Node package managers are configured to use approved registries, package firewalls, or internal artifact proxies.

## Scope
- Check npm, pnpm, Yarn, and Bun registry endpoints against `.pkgwarden.yml` approved registries.
- Detect public registry bypass when a Socket, Veracode, or private-registry profile is selected.
- Detect scoped registry mappings and missing mappings for private package scopes.
- Flag plaintext HTTP registries unless explicitly allowed.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Baseline reports registry posture without requiring a vendor.
- [ ] Socket and Veracode profiles can require configured firewall/proxy endpoints.
- [ ] Findings avoid claiming dashboard policy settings are verified unless API support exists.
- [ ] Tests cover public registry, private proxy, Socket-style proxy URL, and Veracode-style endpoint examples.

## Dependencies
- PW-012
- PW-016
- PW-018
- PW-008

issues.title: PW-021: Detect Python package managers, manifests, lockfiles, and config files
issues.milestone: M2: Python package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement Python ecosystem inventory for pip, uv, and Poetry.

## Scope
- Detect `requirements*.txt`, `constraints*.txt`, `pyproject.toml`, `uv.lock`, `poetry.lock`, `poetry.toml`, `pip.conf`, `pip.ini`, `.pip/pip.conf`, and `.pypirc`.
- Identify likely manager: pip, uv, Poetry, or mixed.
- Handle monorepos with multiple Python project roots.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Inventory identifies pip, uv, and Poetry projects in fixtures.
- [ ] Mixed manager repos produce a warning when configs conflict.
- [ ] Relevant files include source locations and project root association.

## Dependencies
- PW-003
- PW-004

issues.title: PW-022: Parse pip config, requirements, and constraints files
issues.milestone: M2: Python package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Parse pip-oriented files for package index configuration, hash enforcement, pinning, and upload-age controls.

## Scope
- Parse `index-url`, `extra-index-url`, `trusted-host`, `require-hashes`, `no-index`, `find-links`, and `uploaded-prior-to` from config and requirements contexts.
- Parse requirement lines enough to detect pinned/unpinned specs and hashes.
- Preserve line metadata and comments where useful.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Parser handles common requirements syntax, includes, constraints, environment markers, hashes, and comments.
- [ ] Index settings are discoverable from both config files and requirements flags.
- [ ] Tests cover malformed lines, nested requirements includes, and URL credentials with redaction.

## Dependencies
- PW-004
- PW-010
- PW-021

issues.title: PW-023: Implement pip dependency-confusion, hash, and pinning rules
issues.milestone: M2: Python package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Add pip rules that flag unsafe package index topology and missing integrity/pinning controls.

## Scope
- Rule `python.pip.extra_index_url_confusion`: warn on `extra-index-url` unless explicitly approved.
- Rule `python.pip.require_hashes`: recommend/require `--require-hashes` for production requirement sets.
- Rule `python.pip.requirements_pinned`: flag unpinned or broad dependency specs for production contexts.
- Rule `python.pip.trusted_host_plain_http`: flag insecure trusted hosts and HTTP indexes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Findings explain dependency-confusion risk without claiming package vulnerability status.
- [ ] Policy can classify requirement files as production, development, or test.
- [ ] Hash rule handles all-lines-must-be-hashed semantics.
- [ ] Fixtures cover safe private proxy, unsafe `extra-index-url`, and hash-enabled requirements.

## Dependencies
- PW-022
- PW-008

issues.title: PW-024: Parse uv configuration and implement uv cooldown/lockfile rules
issues.milestone: M2: Python package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Support uv projects and validate `exclude-newer`/lockfile posture.

## Scope
- Parse `[tool.uv]` and related uv config from `pyproject.toml` and standalone uv config files where present.
- Detect `uv.lock`.
- Implement rule `uv.cooldown.exclude_newer`.
- Implement rule `uv.lockfile.required` and CI locked-install placeholder.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] uv projects are discovered through `pyproject.toml` and lockfiles.
- [ ] Missing or weak `exclude-newer` produces a configurable finding.
- [ ] Rules include recommendation for aligning Renovate `minimumReleaseAge` with uv `exclude-newer`.
- [ ] Fixtures cover safe and unsafe uv projects.

## Dependencies
- PW-021
- PW-004

issues.title: PW-025: Parse Poetry config and implement Poetry source/cooldown/lockfile rules
issues.milestone: M2: Python package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Support Poetry project hardening, including source configuration and minimum release-age controls where supported.

## Scope
- Parse `pyproject.toml`, `poetry.toml`, and relevant Poetry source settings.
- Detect `poetry.lock`.
- Implement rules for source priority/explicit sources, cooldown/min-release-age where supported by configured Poetry version, and lockfile presence.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Poetry projects are discovered and reported.
- [ ] Unsafe source topology and missing lockfile are flagged.
- [ ] Cooldown rule gracefully handles unknown or unsupported Poetry versions.
- [ ] Fixtures cover private source, PyPI fallback, and lockfile cases.

## Dependencies
- PW-021
- PW-004

issues.title: PW-026: Implement Python registry, firewall, and private-index posture checks
issues.milestone: M2: Python package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Validate whether Python package managers are configured to use approved indexes, package firewalls, or internal artifact proxies.

## Scope
- Check pip, uv, and Poetry index/source endpoints against `.pkgwarden.yml` approved registries.
- Detect public PyPI bypass when a Socket, Veracode, or private-registry profile is selected.
- Flag plaintext HTTP and credentials in URLs.
- Avoid claiming external firewall policy is verified unless an integration exists.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Baseline reports Python index posture.
- [ ] Socket and Veracode profiles can require configured firewall/proxy endpoints.
- [ ] Findings provide precise file/line evidence and safe remediation guidance.
- [ ] Fixtures cover direct PyPI, internal proxy, and firewall endpoint patterns.

## Dependencies
- PW-022
- PW-024
- PW-025
- PW-008

issues.title: PW-027: Implement Python production requirements classification
issues.milestone: M2: Python package-manager hardening MVP
issues.body:
## Summary
Classify requirement and dependency files by likely purpose so strict rules do not overfire on test/dev inputs.

## Scope
- Classify names such as `requirements.txt`, `requirements-prod.txt`, `requirements-dev.txt`, `constraints.txt`, and policy overrides.
- Use classification in pinning/hash/cooldown severity decisions.
- Document classification behavior.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Policy can override file classification.
- [ ] Production files default to stronger checks than dev/test files.
- [ ] Human report shows why a file was treated as production or non-production.

## Dependencies
- PW-023
- PW-008

issues.title: PW-028: Parse Dependabot configuration and implement cooldown rules
issues.milestone: M3: Dependency bots, CI install validation, and firewall posture
issues.body:
## Summary
Parse `.github/dependabot.yml` and validate that dependency updates use cooldown periods aligned with PkgWarden policy.

## Scope
- Parse update entries, ecosystems, directories, schedules, groups, ignore rules, and cooldown settings.
- Implement rule `dependabot.cooldown.required`.
- Detect per-update cooldown coverage gaps for supported ecosystems.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Dependabot config is parsed with file/line evidence.
- [ ] Missing cooldown produces a finding when the ecosystem has package-manager cooldown expectations.
- [ ] Rule can recommend default-days and semver-specific cooldown snippets.
- [ ] Fixtures cover no config, partial config, and aligned config.

## Dependencies
- PW-004
- PW-008

issues.title: PW-029: Parse Renovate configuration and implement minimum-release-age rules
issues.milestone: M3: Dependency bots, CI install validation, and firewall posture
issues.body:
## Summary
Parse Renovate configuration and validate `minimumReleaseAge` usage and alignment with package-manager native cooldowns.

## Scope
- Parse `renovate.json`, `.github/renovate.json`, `renovate.json5` where feasible, and packageRules.
- Implement rule `renovate.minimum_release_age.required`.
- Implement rule `renovate.minimum_release_age.native_alignment`.
- Detect package rules that bypass cooldown.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Renovate config is parsed with useful evidence.
- [ ] Findings distinguish global and package-rule-specific minimum release age.
- [ ] Rules recommend aligning Renovate with npm/pnpm/Yarn/Bun/uv/Poetry settings.
- [ ] Fixtures cover missing, global, package-rule, and bypass cases.

## Dependencies
- PW-004
- PW-013
- PW-015
- PW-017
- PW-018
- PW-024
- PW-025

issues.title: PW-030: Parse GitHub Actions workflows for package-manager install commands
issues.milestone: M3: Dependency bots, CI install validation, and firewall posture
issues.body:
## Summary
Analyze GitHub Actions workflow commands to see whether CI enforces reproducible and hardened dependency installs.

## Scope
- Parse `.github/workflows/*.yml` and identify `run` commands invoking npm, pnpm, Yarn, Bun, pip, uv, and Poetry.
- Detect install command variants such as `npm install`, `npm ci`, `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `yarn install --immutable`, `bun install --frozen-lockfile`, `uv sync --locked`, and `pip install -r`.
- Correlate workflows to package managers discovered in the repo.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Workflow parser identifies install commands and associated file/line evidence.
- [ ] Scanner can report when CI uses non-reproducible install commands.
- [ ] Multiline run blocks and matrix workflows are supported well enough for MVP.
- [ ] Fixtures cover common Node and Python CI workflows.

## Dependencies
- PW-004
- PW-011
- PW-021

issues.title: PW-031: Implement CI locked-install validation rules
issues.milestone: M3: Dependency bots, CI install validation, and firewall posture
issues.body:
## Summary
Add rules that validate CI install commands enforce lockfiles and do not bypass package-manager hardening.

## Scope
- Rule `ci.node.npm_ci_required` for npm projects.
- Rule `ci.node.pnpm_frozen_lockfile`.
- Rule `ci.node.yarn_immutable`.
- Rule `ci.node.bun_frozen_lockfile`.
- Rule `ci.python.uv_locked_sync`.
- Rule `ci.python.poetry_lock_consistency`.
- Advisory rule for pip hash mode in production deploy workflows.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Rules report specific workflows and commands that need changes.
- [ ] Rules are profile-aware and avoid requiring unsupported settings.
- [ ] Recommendations include safer command replacements.
- [ ] Fixtures cover passing and failing CI workflows.

## Dependencies
- PW-030
- PW-013
- PW-015
- PW-017
- PW-018
- PW-023
- PW-024
- PW-025

issues.title: PW-032: Implement firewall posture summary and vendor-neutral evidence model
issues.milestone: M3: Dependency bots, CI install validation, and firewall posture
issues.body:
## Summary
Create a single firewall/proxy posture summary that combines evidence from Node, Python, CI, and policy config.

## Scope
- Summarize whether package acquisition appears direct-to-public, internal-proxy, package-firewall, mixed, or unknown.
- Show per-package-manager evidence.
- Indicate when dashboard/API policy cannot be verified from repo-only data.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Human and JSON reports include a firewall posture section.
- [ ] Socket/Veracode profiles produce clear findings when endpoints are missing.
- [ ] Repo-only limitations are explicitly stated in findings.
- [ ] No finding claims external policy enforcement unless evidence exists.

## Dependencies
- PW-020
- PW-026
- PW-007

issues.title: PW-033: Implement token and plaintext registry hardening rules
issues.milestone: M3: Dependency bots, CI install validation, and firewall posture
issues.body:
## Summary
Flag risky package-manager credential handling and insecure registry transport.

## Scope
- Detect likely committed static tokens in package-manager configs.
- Detect credentials embedded in registry/index URLs.
- Detect plaintext HTTP package sources unless explicitly allowed by policy.
- Recommend environment-variable placeholders and secret managers.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Findings are redacted but actionable.
- [ ] Rules support npm, Yarn, pip, Poetry, uv, and NuGet-ready patterns.
- [ ] Policy can allow known internal HTTP hosts with explicit reason.
- [ ] Tests cover false-positive-resistant token detection.

## Dependencies
- PW-010
- PW-020
- PW-026

issues.title: PW-034: Generate Markdown report suitable for PR comments
issues.milestone: M3: Dependency bots, CI install validation, and firewall posture
issues.body:
## Summary
Add Markdown report output optimized for pull request comments and issue bodies.

## Scope
- Summary table by severity and category.
- Top findings with file paths, evidence snippets, and remediation snippets.
- Firewall posture summary.
- Optional truncation for long reports.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `--format markdown` produces readable output.
- [ ] Report includes links/paths suitable for GitHub PR comments.
- [ ] Long output is truncated deterministically with a note.
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover formatting.

## Dependencies
- PW-007
- PW-032

issues.title: PW-035: Implement SARIF output for GitHub code scanning
issues.milestone: M4: Reporting, GitHub Action, and releaseable v0.1
issues.body:
## Summary
Emit SARIF output so PkgWarden findings can be uploaded to GitHub code scanning and other SARIF consumers.

## Scope
- Map findings to SARIF rules, results, levels, locations, and help text.
- Include stable rule ids and references.
- Redact secrets in SARIF evidence.
- Validate generated SARIF against schema or test fixtures.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `--format sarif` produces valid SARIF for sample findings.
- [ ] GitHub code scanning upload accepts generated output in a fixture workflow.
- [ ] SARIF rule metadata includes category, severity, and remediation summary.
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover at least Node, Python, and CI findings.

## Dependencies
- PW-002
- PW-007
- PW-010

issues.title: PW-036: Create GitHub Action wrapper
issues.milestone: M4: Reporting, GitHub Action, and releaseable v0.1
issues.body:
## Summary
Package PkgWarden as a GitHub Action that can scan repos, upload SARIF, and optionally comment on PRs.

## Scope
- Create `action.yml` inputs for profile, path, format, fail threshold, upload-sarif, comment-pr, and policy-file.
- Run the PkgWarden binary in CI.
- Document required permissions.
- Support pull request and push workflows.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Example workflow runs against fixture repo.
- [ ] Action can produce SARIF artifact and upload it when enabled.
- [ ] Action can emit Markdown summary to job summary.
- [ ] Permissions are least-privilege and documented.

## Dependencies
- PW-035
- PW-034

issues.title: PW-037: Add GitHub Actions annotations output
issues.milestone: M4: Reporting, GitHub Action, and releaseable v0.1
issues.body:
## Summary
Emit GitHub workflow command annotations for findings with file/line evidence.

## Scope
- Map high/medium/low findings to error/warning/notice based on threshold settings.
- Use redacted, short messages.
- Avoid duplicate annotations for the same finding.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Action logs include annotations for fixture findings.
- [ ] Annotations link to the correct file/line where available.
- [ ] Rules without line evidence are summarized without invalid annotations.

## Dependencies
- PW-036

issues.title: PW-038: Create public MVP rule catalog documentation
issues.milestone: M4: Reporting, GitHub Action, and releaseable v0.1
issues.body:
## Summary
Publish a documented rule catalog for v0.1 that explains each rule, rationale, severity, profiles, and remediation.

## Scope
- One page per rule or generated catalog page.
- Include examples of failing and passing config.
- Include profile applicability and autofix availability.
- Include non-goals and limitations.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every v0.1 rule has documentation.
- [ ] Docs can be generated or checked for missing rule ids.
- [ ] Docs avoid implying vulnerability scanning or package risk scoring.
- [ ] Examples are accurate for npm, pnpm, Yarn, Bun, pip, uv, Poetry, Dependabot, and Renovate rules.

## Dependencies
- PW-013
- PW-015
- PW-017
- PW-018
- PW-023
- PW-024
- PW-025
- PW-028
- PW-029
- PW-031
- PW-032

issues.title: PW-039: Add release workflow, versioning, and binary distribution
issues.milestone: M4: Reporting, GitHub Action, and releaseable v0.1
issues.body:
## Summary
Set up repeatable releases for PkgWarden CLI and GitHub Action.

## Scope
- Versioning strategy and changelog.
- Build binaries for major platforms if using a compiled CLI.
- Create GitHub release artifacts.
- Document installation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Release workflow can produce a test release.
- [ ] Checksums are generated for release artifacts.
- [ ] README installation instructions work against the release artifacts.
- [ ] Action versioning strategy is documented.

## Dependencies
- PW-036

issues.title: PW-040: Build v0.1 end-to-end demo fixtures and smoke tests
issues.milestone: M4: Reporting, GitHub Action, and releaseable v0.1
issues.body:
## Summary
Create representative demo repositories and end-to-end smoke tests for the full v0.1 scope.

## Scope
- Unsafe Node repo with npm/pnpm/Yarn/Bun examples.
- Unsafe Python repo with pip/uv/Poetry examples.
- Repo with Socket/Veracode/private-registry profile expectations.
- Repo with Dependabot, Renovate, and GitHub Actions workflows.
- Expected human, JSON, Markdown, and SARIF outputs.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] E2E tests run in CI.
- [ ] At least one demo repo produces actionable high/medium findings.
- [ ] At least one demo repo passes baseline with no high findings.
- [ ] Smoke tests cover CLI and GitHub Action invocation.

## Dependencies
- PW-035
- PW-036
- PW-038

issues.title: PW-041: Publish v0.1 launch README and quickstart
issues.milestone: M4: Reporting, GitHub Action, and releaseable v0.1
issues.body:
## Summary
Prepare user-facing docs for the first public release.

## Scope
- Quickstart for local CLI.
- Quickstart for GitHub Action.
- Example reports.
- Recommended baseline policy.
- FAQ explaining relationship to SCA, package firewalls, Scorecard, and dependency bots.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New users can run a scan in under five commands.
- [ ] Docs include one baseline and one strict profile example.
- [ ] FAQ clearly states how PkgWarden complements SCA and package firewalls.

## Dependencies
- PW-038
- PW-039
- PW-040

issues.title: PW-042: Implement patch/diff model for safe autofix
issues.milestone: M5: Safe autofix and policy profiles v0.2
issues.body:
## Summary
Create a general patch model that can propose safe config changes without immediately mutating files.

## Scope
- Represent file edits with before/after context.
- Support `--fix --dry-run` and `--fix --apply`.
- Classify fixes as safe, review-required, or unsupported.
- Prevent edits that would insert secrets or unknown registry endpoints.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Dry-run prints or writes a unified diff.
- [ ] Apply mode modifies files only when explicitly requested.
- [ ] Conflicting/malformed files produce review-required results.
- [ ] Autofix metadata is included in JSON output.

## Dependencies
- PW-002
- PW-004
- PW-007

issues.title: PW-043: Add safe autofix for npm cooldown and lockfile config
issues.milestone: M5: Safe autofix and policy profiles v0.2
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement conservative fixes for npm settings that can be safely suggested without org-specific secrets.

## Scope
- Add or update `min-release-age` in `.npmrc`.
- Avoid modifying registry or auth settings unless explicit approved registry policy is provided.
- Optionally set `package-lock=true` if explicitly disabled.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Dry-run diff adds `min-release-age=<policy default>` when missing.
- [ ] Fix preserves comments where feasible.
- [ ] Fix refuses to overwrite unknown conflicting values without review.

## Dependencies
- PW-042
- PW-013

issues.title: PW-044: Add safe autofix for pnpm, Yarn, and Bun cooldown settings
issues.milestone: M5: Safe autofix and policy profiles v0.2
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement safe patch generation for native Node package-manager cooldown/minimum-age controls.

## Scope
- pnpm: add or update `minimumReleaseAge` in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`.
- Yarn: add or update `npmMinimalAgeGate` in `.yarnrc.yml`.
- Bun: add or update `install.minimumReleaseAge` in `bunfig.toml`.
- Treat build-script changes as review-required, not auto-apply by default.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Dry-run diffs are valid YAML/TOML.
- [ ] Apply mode preserves valid config and passes parser round-trip tests.
- [ ] Autofix never enables broad script execution.

## Dependencies
- PW-042
- PW-015
- PW-017
- PW-018

issues.title: PW-045: Add safe autofix for Dependabot and Renovate cooldowns
issues.milestone: M5: Safe autofix and policy profiles v0.2
issues.body:
## Summary
Generate safe bot config changes to add missing cooldown/minimum-release-age settings.

## Scope
- Dependabot: add `cooldown` to applicable update entries.
- Renovate: add global or packageRule `minimumReleaseAge` based on policy.
- Avoid changing schedules, groups, or ignored dependencies unless explicitly requested.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Dry-run diffs show minimal bot config changes.
- [ ] Fix handles missing files by proposing starter configs when policy allows.
- [ ] Renovate fixes respect existing packageRules and avoid duplicate settings.

## Dependencies
- PW-042
- PW-028
- PW-029

issues.title: PW-046: Define and validate policy profiles
issues.milestone: M5: Safe autofix and policy profiles v0.2
issues.body:
## Summary
Codify built-in profiles and make profile behavior transparent and testable.

## Scope
- Profiles: baseline, strict, socket-firewall, veracode-package-firewall, private-registry, regulated-ci, oss-maintainer.
- For each profile, define enabled rules, default severities, required/optional controls, and default cooldown.
- Add tests that verify rule/profile matrix.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Profile matrix is generated into docs.
- [ ] Selecting each profile changes rule requirements predictably.
- [ ] Users can extend or override profile values in `.pkgwarden.yml`.

## Dependencies
- PW-008
- PW-005
- PW-038

issues.title: PW-047: Implement config precedence resolver
issues.milestone: M5: Safe autofix and policy profiles v0.2
issues.body:
## Summary
Resolve precedence between CLI flags, `.pkgwarden.yml`, built-in profiles, environment variables, and defaults.

## Scope
- Define deterministic precedence order.
- Show effective policy in JSON output when requested.
- Warn on contradictory settings.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Precedence behavior is documented.
- [ ] Tests cover CLI overriding policy and policy overriding profile defaults.
- [ ] Contradictions produce actionable warnings.

## Dependencies
- PW-008
- PW-046

issues.title: PW-048: Add Maven and Gradle hardening support
issues.milestone: M6: Additional ecosystems v0.3
issues.body:
## Summary
Expand PkgWarden to JVM build tools with repository centralization, mirror/proxy posture, dependency verification, and lockfile checks.

## Scope
- Maven: parse `pom.xml` and relevant `settings.xml` patterns; flag public fallback and recommend repository manager mirror posture.
- Gradle: parse `settings.gradle(.kts)` and `build.gradle(.kts)` enough to detect repositories and dependency verification metadata.
- Rules for Gradle `FAIL_ON_PROJECT_REPOS`, dependency verification metadata, and repository centralization.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] JVM projects are discovered and reported.
- [ ] Rules distinguish direct public repos from internal repository managers.
- [ ] Fixtures cover Maven mirror, Gradle centralized repos, and unsafe project-level repos.

## Dependencies
- PW-005
- PW-046

issues.title: PW-049: Add NuGet hardening support
issues.milestone: M6: Additional ecosystems v0.3
issues.body:
## Summary
Support NuGet package-source mapping, lockfiles, and private feed posture.

## Scope
- Parse `nuget.config`, `packages.lock.json`, and relevant project files.
- Rules for package source mapping, locked restore, plaintext feeds, and credentials.
- Private-registry profile support.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] NuGet projects are discovered.
- [ ] Findings identify missing package source mapping for mixed feeds.
- [ ] Fixtures cover safe and unsafe NuGet configs.

## Dependencies
- PW-005
- PW-010
- PW-046

issues.title: PW-050: Add Bundler/RubyGems hardening support
issues.milestone: M6: Additional ecosystems v0.3
issues.body:
## Summary
Support Ruby Bundler hardening for mirrors, fallback behavior, lockfiles, and checksums.

## Scope
- Parse `Gemfile`, `Gemfile.lock`, and `.bundle/config`.
- Rules for mirror/fallback timeout posture, lockfile presence, and lockfile checksums where supported.
- Private-registry and package-firewall profile support.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Bundler projects are discovered.
- [ ] Findings include actionable mirror and checksum recommendations.
- [ ] Fixtures cover direct RubyGems, internal mirror, fallback timeout, and checksum cases.

## Dependencies
- PW-005
- PW-046

issues.title: PW-051: Add Cargo hardening support
issues.milestone: M6: Additional ecosystems v0.3
issues.body:
## Summary
Support Cargo source replacement, registry posture, lockfiles, and git dependency detection.

## Scope
- Parse `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, and `.cargo/config.toml`.
- Rules for source replacement, private registries, git/path dependencies, and lockfile presence for applications.
- Socket Firewall profile support where applicable.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Cargo projects are discovered.
- [ ] Rules flag git dependencies and public registry bypass under private-registry profile.
- [ ] Fixtures cover default crates.io, source replacement, and git dependency cases.

## Dependencies
- PW-005
- PW-046

issues.title: PW-052: Add Go modules hardening support
issues.milestone: M6: Additional ecosystems v0.3
issues.body:
## Summary
Support Go module proxy, checksum database, and private module posture checks.

## Scope
- Parse `go.mod`, `go.sum`, and repo-local Go environment/config hints where available.
- Rules for `GOPROXY`, `GOSUMDB`, `GOPRIVATE`, `GONOSUMDB`, and missing `go.sum`.
- Warn when checksum verification is disabled without policy rationale.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Go modules are discovered.
- [ ] Rules explain private module proxy/sumdb tradeoffs.
- [ ] Fixtures cover public, private, and disabled checksum database scenarios.

## Dependencies
- PW-005
- PW-046

issues.title: PW-053: Design plugin interface for custom rules and parsers
issues.milestone: M7: Organization scale and plugin system v1.0
issues.body:
## Summary
Produce and prototype a plugin interface that lets organizations add custom package-manager hardening rules without forking PkgWarden.

## Scope
- Evaluate in-process vs external plugin models.
- Define stable contracts for inventory access, findings, rules, and policy.
- Prototype one custom rule plugin.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Architecture decision record is committed.
- [ ] Prototype demonstrates a custom rule running against scanner inventory.
- [ ] Security risks of plugin execution are documented.

## Dependencies
- PW-046

issues.title: PW-054: Spike Socket Firewall API adapter
issues.milestone: M7: Organization scale and plugin system v1.0
issues.body:
## Summary
Research and prototype optional Socket API usage to validate org-level firewall policy settings that cannot be proven from repo files alone.

## Scope
- Identify API surfaces needed to verify cooldown policy and firewall mode.
- Define auth requirements and least-privilege token handling.
- Prototype read-only validation if feasible.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision memo explains what can and cannot be verified through the API.
- [ ] Prototype redacts all tokens and fails closed when auth is unavailable.
- [ ] No repo-only rule depends on this integration.

## Dependencies
- PW-032
- PW-046

issues.title: PW-055: Spike Veracode Package Firewall API adapter
issues.milestone: M7: Organization scale and plugin system v1.0
issues.body:
## Summary
Research and prototype optional Veracode Package Firewall API usage to validate external policy posture beyond local package-manager config.

## Scope
- Identify API surfaces for package firewall policy/rules validation.
- Define auth requirements and safe token handling.
- Prototype read-only validation if feasible.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision memo explains what can and cannot be verified through the API.
- [ ] Prototype does not expose secrets.
- [ ] Repo-only Veracode profile remains usable without API credentials.

## Dependencies
- PW-032
- PW-046

issues.title: PW-056: Add org-level policy bundles and baseline export
issues.milestone: M7: Organization scale and plugin system v1.0
issues.body:
## Summary
Allow organizations to publish reusable policy bundles that repos can consume or vendor locally.

## Scope
- Policy bundle format for approved registries, profile defaults, rule overrides, and suppressions.
- Support local file and URL-based bundle references if safe.
- Export effective policy for audit.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Org bundle can be applied to a fixture repo.
- [ ] Effective policy export includes source of each setting.
- [ ] Remote bundle support is disabled by default or protected against unsafe fetch behavior.

## Dependencies
- PW-047

issues.title: PW-057: Create contributor onboarding and maintainer workflow
issues.milestone: M7: Organization scale and plugin system v1.0
issues.body:
## Summary
Prepare PkgWarden for external contributors and maintainers.

## Scope
- Contribution guide.
- Rule authoring guide.
- Fixture authoring guide.
- Maintainer release checklist.
- Security issue reporting process.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A new contributor can add a simple rule using docs alone.
- [ ] Security disclosure path is documented.
- [ ] Maintainer checklist includes release and rule-doc validation.

## Dependencies
- PW-038
- PW-039

issues.title: PW-058: Create governance and trademark/name usage note for PkgWarden
issues.milestone: M7: Organization scale and plugin system v1.0
issues.body:
## Summary
Document basic project governance, name usage, and ownership expectations for Ozark Security Labs.

## Scope
- Maintainer roles.
- Decision process for adding rules/profiles.
- Name/logo usage note.
- License finalization.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Governance document is committed.
- [ ] License choice is explicit.
- [ ] Project ownership and maintainership are clear.

## Dependencies
- PW-057
